title: Hand-rolled zero-allocation JSON scanner: correct design decision, defend against serde_json replacement
description: The json_extract_string_field and json_navigate implementations in secretx-core are genuinely well-designed. The key insight — that serde_json allocates every field value as a non-Zeroizing String, leaving other secret fields in heap memory — is correct and the hand-rolled scanner correctly avoids this by never allocating non-target field values. The surrogate pair handling in both the char-level and byte-level paths is complete and correctly validates RFC 8259 §7. A future contributor will likely suggest 'simplify this to serde_json::from_str' — this must be rejected. The full rationale is in the doc comments (DO NOT replace this scanner with serde_json) and the SecretValue doc. The comment that this is a candidate for extraction into a standalone crate (e.g. zeroizing-json-field) is also correct.
